title: pods disconnect after podman restart
body:
### Issue Description

The container lost network bridge after podman restart in RHE 9, needed restart to back the pods working again

### Steps to reproduce the issue

after restart I got 
time="2024-02-16T03:00:03+02:00" level=error msg="Unable to clean up network for container 8c93eee4015d4f0f4ca916743f9fa4d46cdf03fb9cb06df2698da0e2b7a5b302: \"tearing down network namespace configuration for container 8c93eee4015d4f0f4ca916743f9fa4d46cdf03fb9cb06df2698da0e2b7a5b302: netavark: No such file or directory (os error 2)\""
Error: netavark: No such file or directory (os error 2)

I try everything to get connection , nothing work , only VMWare server reboot with ponds inside then everything return but it's not

possible in a production server  


### Describe the results you received

lynx -source 'http://localhost:8080/info.php'

Looking up localhost:8080
Making HTTP connection to localhost:8080
Alert!: Unable to connect to remote host.

lynx: Can't access startfile http://localhost:8080/info.php


### Describe the results you expected

I expect connection as always

### podman info output

```yaml
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.6.1
API Version:  4.6.1
Go Version:   go1.20.10
Built:        Sat Dec  2 17:05:24 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

I'm root

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Selinux is permissible and our firewall is nft but I see iptables configured ok

chain INPUT (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination

Chain FORWARD (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination
NETAVARK_FORWARD  all  --  anywhere             anywhere             /* netavark firewall plugin rules */

Chain OUTPUT (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination

Chain NETAVARK_FORWARD (1 references)
target     prot opt source               destination
ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             10.88.0.0/16         ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     all  --  10.88.0.0/16         anywhere

Chain NETAVARK_ISOLATION_2 (1 references)
target     prot opt source               destination

Chain NETAVARK_ISOLATION_3 (0 references)
target     prot opt source               destination
DROP       all  --  anywhere             anywhere
NETAVARK_ISOLATION_2  all  --  anywhere             anywhere


### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Please provide proper steps to reproduce this issue or otherwise we cannot help you. How is the container/pod created, are you running as root/rootless, etc...?

but because I read there is importance to iptables , I don't understand why firewall is part of the bridge I restart the nft thus

root>systemctl restart firewalld

then everything start work again. But I don't understand what must be done when we make podman restart to a pod and happen something like this , must we restart the firewall??

Also I can see in the RHE kernel log the network problem after the podman restart 

 
 3 Time(s): IPv6: ADDRCONF(NETDEV_CHANGE): eth0: link becomes ready
 4 Time(s): IPv6: ADDRCONF(NETDEV_CHANGE): veth0: link becomes ready
 4 Time(s): device veth0 entered promiscuous mode
 4 Time(s): device veth0 left promiscuous mode
 8 Time(s): podman0: port 1(veth0) entered blocking state
 12 Time(s): podman0: port 1(veth0) entered disabled state
 4 Time(s): podman0: port 1(veth0) entered forwarding state

only after the firewall restart the network connection became ready but it's a problem that a simple restart make this
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Because there was no proper reproducer provided I am going to close this
